mechanism_narrative: Lower levels of the federal funds rate reduce equity discount rates, which increases the present value of future corporate cash flows and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of observations when FEDFUNDS is below 1%, or the Rank IC between FEDFUNDS levels and 20-day returns is positive over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points over two months signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending and corporate revenue expectations, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: When UNRATE rises more than 0.5pp over 2 months, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in greater than 55% of instances, or the mean return in triggered periods is not significantly below zero (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in retail sales concurrent with a month-over-month increase in industrial production signals a demand-supply mismatch where inventory builds and corporate margins compress, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS declines MoM while INDPRO rises MoM
failure_condition: Joint occurrences of RSAFS declining while INDPRO rises do not predict negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances, or the interaction term adds no incremental explanatory power (F-test p > 0.05) beyond individual series in a rolling 3-year regression.

mechanism_narrative: In a high inflation regime, elevated CPIAUCSL year-over-year growth forces a monetary policy response that raises real discount rates and compresses equity multiples, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: During periods when CPIAUCSL YoY exceeds 4%, S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of observations, or the average return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) relative to non-high-inflation periods.

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive monthly declines in industrial production indicate a contracting manufacturing sector that reduces aggregate corporate earnings and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Following two consecutive monthly declines in INDPRO, S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in greater than 50% of instances, or the mean forward return is not significantly below zero (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly increases in real GDP reflect aggregate demand expansion that drives corporate revenue growth and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days as earnings expectations reprice upward.
failure_condition: The Rank IC between GDPC1 changes and 60-day forward returns is negative over any 3-year rolling window, or positive GDP growth coincides with negative 60-day returns in more than 50% of quarters.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated housing starts above 1000 thousand units combined with federal funds rates below 1% signal strong housing demand fueled by cheap credit, which drives construction employment and materials demand that lifts broad corporate profits and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When HOUST exceeds 1000 and FEDFUNDS is below 1%, S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of joint occurrences, or a linear model with the interaction term shows no significant coefficient (p > 0.05) in a rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Three consecutive months of payroll employment gains exceeding 200 thousand signal accelerating labor market tightness that boosts wage and consumption growth expectations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: When PAYEMS increases by more than 200k for 3 consecutive months, S&P 500 5-day forward returns are negative in greater than 50% of triggered instances, or the mean forward return is not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05).
